author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …The Title of this Psalm thus speaketh: “At the end, corrupt not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “corrupt not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which Thou hast promised, perform.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this then let the mind’s eye be directed, “unto the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let all the things which have occurred in the way be passed over, in order that we may attain to the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let proud men exult because of present felicity, let them swell with honours, glitter in gold, overflow with domestics, be encircled with the services of clients: these things pass away, they pass away like a shadow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When that end shall have come, when all who now hope in the Lord are to rejoice, then to them shall come sorrow without end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the meek shall have received that which the proud deride, then the vapouring of the proud shall be turned into mourning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then shall there be that voice which we know in the Book of Wisdom: for they shall say at that time when they see the glory of the Saints, who, when they were in humiliation, endured them; who, when they were exalted, consented not—at that time then they shall say, “These are they whom sometime we have had in derision.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where they also say, “What hath pride profited us, and the boasting of riches hath bestowed upon us what?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things have passed away like a shadow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because on things corruptible they relied, their hope shall be corrupted: but our own hope at that time shall be substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in order that the promise of God may remain whole and sure and certain towards us, we have said out of a heart of faith, “at the end corrupt not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not, therefore, lest any mighty man should corrupt the promises of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not corrupt, because He is truthful; He hath no one more mighty by whom His promise may be corrupted: let us be then sure concerning the promises of God; and let us sing now from the place where the Psalm beginneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We will confess to Thee, O Lord, we will confess to Thee, and will invoke Thy name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not invoke, before thou confess: confess, and invoke.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Him whom thou art invoking, unto thyself thou callest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is it to invoke, but unto thyself to call?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He is invoked by thee, that is, if He is called to thee, unto whom doth He draw near?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To a proud man He draweth not near.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: High indeed He is, one lifted up attaineth not unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In order that we may reach all exalted objects, we raise ourselves, and if we are not able to reach them, we look for some appliances or ladders, in order that being exalted we may reach exalted objects: contrariwise God is both high, and by the lowly He is reached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is written, “Nigh is the Lord to them that have bruised the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bruising of the heart is Godliness, humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that bruiseth himself is angry with himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him make himself angry in order that he may make Him merciful; let him make himself judge, in order that he may make Him Advocate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore God doth come when invoked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto whom doth He come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the proud man He cometh not.…Take heed therefore what ye do: for if He knoweth, He is not unobservant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is better therefore that He be unobservant than known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is that same being unobservant, but not knowing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, not to know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to animadvert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even as the act of one avenging animadversion is wont to be spoken of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here one praying that He be unobservant: “Turn away Thy face from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then wilt thou do if He shall have turned away His face from thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A grievous thing it is, and to be feared, lest He forsake thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, if He turn not away His face, He animadverteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God knoweth this thing, God can do this thing, namely, both turn away face from one sinning, and not turn away from one confessing.…Confess therefore and invoke.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by confessing thou purgest the Temple, into which He may come, when invoked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confess and invoke.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He turn away face from thy sins, not turn away from thee: turn away face from that which thou hast wrought, not turn away from that which He hath Himself wrought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thee, as man, He hath Himself wrought, thy sins thou hast thyself wrought.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that there is a strengthening of the sense in repetition, by many passages of the Scriptures we are taught.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is that which the Lord saith, “Verily, Verily.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence in certain Psalms is, “So be it, So be it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To signify the thing, one “So be it” would have been sufficient: to signify confirmation, there hath been added another “So be it.”…Countless passages of such sort there are throughout all the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With these it is sufficient that we have commended to your notice a way of speaking which ye may observe in all like cases: now to the substance attend: “We will confess to Thee,” he saith, “and we will invoke.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have said why before invocation confession doth precede: because whom thou dost invoke, him thou dost invite.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he willeth not to come when invoked, if thou shalt have been lifted up: lifted up if thou shalt have been, thou wilt not be able to confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thou deniest not any things to God that He knoweth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore thy confession doth not teach Him, but it purgeth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4. …Hear ye now the words of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these seemed not as it were to be His words, “We will confess to Thee, O God, we will confess to Thee, and will invoke Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now beginneth the discourse in the person of the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whether Head speaketh or whether members speak, Christ speaketh: He speaketh in the person of the Head, He speaketh in the person of the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what hath been said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall be two in one flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is a great Sacrament:” “I,” he saith, “speak in Christ and in the Church.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He Himself in the Gospel, “Therefore no longer two, but one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in order that ye may know these in a manner to be two persons, and again one by the bond of marriage, as one He speaketh in Isaiah, and saith, “As upon a Bridegroom he hath bound upon me a mitre, and as a Bride he hath clothed me with an ornament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Bridegroom He hath called Himself in the Head, a Bride in the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is speaking therefore as One, let us hear Him, and in Him let us also speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be the members of Him, in order that this voice may possibly be ours also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will tell forth,” he saith, “all Thy marvellous things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ is preaching Himself, He is preaching Himself even in His members now existing, in order that He may guide unto Him others, and they may draw near that were not, and may be united with those members of Him, through which members of Him the Gospel hath been preached; and there may be made one Body under one Head, in one Spirit, in one Life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When I shall have received,” he saith, “the time, I will judge justices” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall He judge justices?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When He shall have received the time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet is the precise time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to His mercy: He first preacheth justices, and then He judgeth justices.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He willed to judge before He willed to preach, who would be found that should be delivered: who would meet Him that should be absolved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore is the time of preaching: “I will tell,” he saith, “all Thy marvellous works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Him telling, hear Him preaching: for if thou shalt have despised Him, “when I shall have received the time,” He saith, “I will judge justices.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I forgive, He saith, now sins to one confessing, I will not spare hereafter one despising.…He hath received a time as Son of Man; He doth govern times as Son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear how as Son of Man He hath received the time of judging.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith in the Gospel, “He hath given to Him power to execute judgment, because Son of Man He is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: According to His nature as Son of God, He hath never received power of judging, because He never lacked the power of judging: according to His nature as Son of Man He hath received a time, as of being born, and of suffering, as of dying, and of rising again, and of ascending, so of coming and of judging.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him His Body also saith these words, for not without them He will judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He saith in the Gospel, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore whole Christ saith, that is, Head and Body in the Saints, “when I shall have received the time, I will judge justices.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The earth hath flowed down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the earth hath flowed down, whence hath it flowed down except by sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore also they are called delinquencies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To delinquish is as it were by a kind of liquidity to slip down from the stability of firmness in virtue and righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is through desire of lower things that every man sinneth: as he is strengthened by the love of higher things, so he falleth down and as it were melteth away by desire of lower things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This flux of things by the sins of man the merciful forgiver observing, being a merciful forgiver of sins, not yet an exactor of punishments, He observeth and saith: The earth herself indeed hath flowed down by them that dwell in her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which followeth is an exposition, not an addition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though thou wert saying, in what manner hath the earth flowed down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have the foundations been withdrawn, and hath anything therein been swallowed up in a sort of gulf?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What I mean by earth is all they that dwell therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have found, he saith, the earth sinful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And I have done what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have strengthened the pillars thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the pillars which He hath strengthened?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pillars He hath called the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So the Apostle Paul concerning his fellow-Apostles saith, “who seemed to be pillars.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what would those pillars have been, except by Him they had been strengthened?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on occasion of a sort of earthquake even these very pillars rocked: at the Passion of the Lord all the Apostles despaired.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore those pillars which rocked at the Passion of the Lord, by the Resurrection were strengthened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Beginning of the building hath cried out through the pillars thereof, and in all those pillars the Architect Himself hath cried out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostle Paul was one pillar of them when he said, “Would ye receive a proof of Him that speaketh in me—Christ?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “I,” he saith, “have strengthened the pillars thereof:” I have risen again, I have shown that death is not to be feared, I have shown to them that fear, that not even the body itself doth perish in the dying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There terrified them wounds, there strengthened them scars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus could have risen again without any scar: for what great matter were it for that power, to restore the frame of the body to such perfect soundness, as that no trace at all of past wound should appear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had power whence He might make it whole even without scar: but He willed to have that whereby He might strengthen the rocking pillars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have heard now, brethren, that which day by day is not kept secret: let us hear now what He hath cried through these pillars.…He crieth what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have said to unjust men, Do not unjustly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4).…But already they have done, and they are guilty: already there hath flowed down the earth, and all they that dwell therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pricked to the heart were they that crucified Christ, they acknowledged their sin, they learned something of the Apostle, that they might not despair of the pardon of the Preacher.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as Physician He had come, and therefore had not come to the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there is no need,” He saith, “to the whole of a physician, but to them that are sick.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not come to call righteous men, but sinners to repentance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “I have said to unjust men, Do not unjustly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They heard not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of old to us it was spoken: we heard not, we fell, were made mortal, were begotten mortal: the earth flowed down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them hear the Physician even now in order that they may rise, Him that came to the sick man, Him whom they would not hear when whole in order that they might not fall, let them hear when lying down in order that they may rise.…“I have said to unjust men, Do not unjustly; and to the delinquent, Do not exalt your horn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall be exalted in you the horn of Christ, if your horn be not exalted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Your horn is of iniquity, the horn of Christ is of majesty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be not therefore lifted up: speak not iniquity against God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5).…What saith He in another Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These things thou hast done,” having enumerated certain sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These things thou hast done,” He saith, “and was silent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “I was silent”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is never silent with commandment, but meanwhile He is silent with punishment: He is keeping still from vengeance, He doth not pronounce sentence against the condemned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this man saith thus, I have done such and such things, and God hath not taken vengeance; behold I am whole, nought of ill hath befallen me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These things thou hast done, and I was silent: thou hast suspected iniquity, that I shall be like unto thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “that I shall be like unto thee”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou art unjust, even Me thou hast deemed unjust; as though an approver of thy misdeeds, and no adversary, no avenger thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what afterwards saith He to thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will convict thee, and will set thee before thine own face”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because now by sinning behind thy back thou settest thyself, seest not thyself, examinest not thyself; I will set thee before thyself, and will bring upon thee punishment from thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also here, “Speak not iniquity against God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many men speak this iniquity; but dare not openly, lest as blasphemers they be abhorred by godly men: in their heart they gnaw upon these things, within they feed upon such impious food; it delighteth them to speak against God, and if they break not out with tongue, in heart they are not silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence in another Psalm is said, “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fool hath said, but he hath feared men: he would not say it where men might hear; and he said it in that place where He might Himself hear concerning whom he said it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore here also in this Psalm (dearly beloved attend), whereas that which He said, “Do not speak iniquity against God,” this He saw many men do in heart, He hath also added, “for neither from East, nor from West, nor from the deserts of the mountains (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6), for God is Judge” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of thine iniquities God is Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If God He is, everywhere He is present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither wilt thou take thyself away from the eyes of God, so that in some quarter thou mayest speak that which He may not hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If from the East God judgeth, withdraw into the West, and say what thou wilt against God: if from the West, go into the East, and there speak: if from the deserts of the mountains He judgeth, go into the midst of the peoples, where thou mayest murmur to thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From no place judgeth He that everywhere is secret, everywhere open; whom it is allowed no one to know as He is, and whom no one is permitted not to know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take heed what thou doest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art speaking iniquity against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Spirit of the Lord hath filled the round world” (another Scripture saith this), “and that which containeth all things hath knowledge of the voice: wherefore he that speaketh unjust things cannot be hid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not therefore think God to be in places: He is with thee such an one as thou shalt have been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is such an one as thou shalt have been?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Good, if thou shalt have been good; and evil to thee He will seem, if evil thou shalt have been; but a Helper, if good thou shalt have been; an Avenger, if evil thou shalt have been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There thou hast a Judge in thy secret place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Willing to do something of evil, from the public thou retirest into thy house, where no enemy may see; from those places of thine house which are open and before the eyes of men, thou removest thyself into a chamber; thou fearest even in thy chamber some witness from some other quarter, thou retirest into thy heart, there thou meditatest: He is more inward than thy heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whithersoever therefore thou shalt have fled, there He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From thyself whither wilt thou flee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou not follow thyself whithersoever thou shalt flee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since there is One more inward even than thyself, there is no place whither thou mayest flee from God angry, but to God reconciled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no place at all whither thou mayest flee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou flee from Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Flee to Him.…What then shall we do now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us come before His face,” ἐν ἐξομολογήσει, come before in confession: He shall come gentle whom thou hadst made angry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither from the deserts of the mountains, for God is Judge:” not from the East, not from the West, not from the deserts of the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God is Judge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If in any place He were, He would not be God: but because God is Judge, not man, do not expect Him out of places.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His place thou wilt be, if thou art good, if after having confessed thou shalt have invoked Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “One He humbleth, and another He exalteth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom humbleth, whom exalteth this Judge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe these two men in the temple, and ye see whom He humbleth and whom He exalteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They went up into the Temple to pray,” He saith, “the one a Pharisee, and the other a Publican.…“Verily I say unto you, that Publican went down justified more than that Pharisee: for every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus hath been explained a verse of this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God the Judge doth what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “One He humbleth, and another He exalteth:” He humbleth the proud, He exalteth the humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the cup in the hand of the Lord of pure wine is full of mixed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He hath poured out of this upon this man; nevertheless, the dreg thereof hath not been emptied; there shall drink all the sinners of earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be somewhat recruited; there is here some obscurity.…The first question that meeteth us is this, “of pure wine it is full of mixed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “of pure,” if “of mixed”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when he saith, “the cup in the hand of the Lord” (to men instructed in the Church of Christ I am speaking), ye ought not indeed to paint in your heart God as it were circumscribed with a human form, lest, though the temples are shut up, ye forge images in your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This cup therefore doth signify something.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will find out this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “in the hand of the Lord,” is, in the power of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the hand of God is spoken of for the power of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even in reference to men ofttimes is said, in hand he hath it: that is, in his power he hath it, when he chooseth he doth it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of pure wine it is full of mixed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In continuation he hath himself explained: “He hath inclined,” he saith, “from this unto this man; nevertheless the dreg thereof hath not been emptied.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold how it was full of mixed wine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it not therefore terrify you that it is both pure and mixed: pure because of the genuineness thereof, mixed because of the dreg.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then in that place is the wine, and what the dreg?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is, “He hath inclined from this unto this man,” in such sort that the dreg thereof was not emptied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Call ye to mind from whence he came to this: “one He humbleth, and another He exalteth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which was figured to us in the Gospel through two men, a Pharisee and a Publican, this let us, taking in a wider sense, understand of two peoples, of Jews and of Gentiles: the people of the Jews that Pharisee was, the people of the Gentiles that Publican.…As those by being proud have withdrawn, so these by confessing have drawn near.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The cup therefore full of pure wine in the hand of the Lord, as far as the Lord giveth me to understand,…the cup of pure wine full of the mixed, seemeth to me to be the Law, which was given to the Jews, and all that Scripture of the Old Testament, as it is called; there are the weights of all manner of sentences.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therein the New Testament lieth concealed, as though in the dreg of corporal Sacraments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The circumcision of the flesh is a thing of great mystery, and there is understood from thence the circumcision of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Temple of Jerusalem is a thing of great mystery, and there is understood from it the Body of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The land of promise is understood to be the Kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sacrifice of victims and of beasts hath a great mystery: but in all those kinds of sacrifices is understood that one Sacrifice and only victim of the Cross, the Lord, instead of all which sacrifices we have one; because even those figured these, that is, with those these were figured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That people received the Law, they received commandments just and good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is so just as, thou shalt not kill, thou shalt not commit adultery, thou shalt not steal, thou shalt not speak false testimony, honour thy father and mother, thou shalt not covet the property of thy neighbour, one God thou shalt adore, and Him alone thou shalt serve, all these things belong to the wine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those things carnal have as it were sunk down in order that they might remain with them, and there might be poured forth from thence all the spiritual understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “the cup in the hand of the Lord,” that is, in the power of the Lord: “of pure wine,” that is, of the mere Law: “is full of mixed,” that is, is together with the dreg of corporal Sacraments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because the one He humbleth, the proud Jew, and the other He exalteth, the confessing Gentile; “He hath inclined from this unto this,” that is, from the Jewish people unto the Gentile people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath inclined what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hath distilled from thence a spiritual sense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nevertheless, the dreg thereof hath not been emptied,” for all the carnal Sacraments have remained with the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall drink all the sinners of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who shall drink?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the sinners of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the sinners of the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews were indeed sinners, but proud: again, the Gentiles were sinners, but humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All sinners shall drink, but see, who the dreg, who the wine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those by drinking the dreg have come to nought: these by drinking the wine have been justified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would dare to speak of them even as inebriated, and I shall not fear: and O that all ye were thus inebriated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Call to mind, “Thy cup inebriating, how passing beautiful!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye think, my brethren, that all those who by confessing Christ even willed to die, were sober?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So drunk they were, that they knew not their friends.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All their kindred, who strove to divert them from the hope of Heavenly rewards by earthly allurements, were not acknowledged, were not heard by them drunken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they not drunken, whose heart had been changed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they not drunken, whose mind had been alienated from this world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall drink,” he saith, “all the sinners of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who shall drink the wine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sinners shall drink, but in order that they may not remain sinners; in order that they may be justified, in order that they may not be punished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I,” for all drink, but separately I, that is, Christ with His Body, “for ever will rejoice, I will Psalm to the God of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): in that promise to be at the end, whereof is said, “corrupt not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all the horns of sinners I will break, and there shall be exalted the horns of the Just” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is, the one He humbleth, the other He exalteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sinners would not have their horns to be broken, which without doubt will be broken at the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not have Him then break them, do thou to-day break them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou hast heard above, do not despise it: “I have said to unjust men, Do not unjustly, and to the delinquents, Do not exalt the horn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou hast heard, do not exalt the horn, thou hast despised and hast exalted the horn: thou shalt come to the end, where there shall come to pass, “All the horns of sinners I will break, and there shall be exalted the horns of the Just.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The horns of sinners are the dignities of proud men: the horns of the Just are the gifts of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by horns exultations are understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hatest on earth earthly exultation, in order that thou mayest have the heavenly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou lovest the earthly, He doth not admit thee to the Heavenly: and unto confusion will belong thy horn which is broken, just as unto glory it will belong, if thy horn is exalted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore there is time for making choice, then there will not be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not say, I will be let go and will make choice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there have preceded the words, “I have said to the unjust.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If I have not said, make ready an excuse, make ready a defence: but if I have said, seize first upon confession, lest thou come unto damnation; for then confession will be too late, and there will be no defence.
